title: The Echo Recursion Chamber

features.name: calling-voices
features.desc-on-touch: You hear the archive speaking to itself. Each voice bounces off the chamber walls and arrives distorted—the meaning shifts with every echo. The archive has learned to compare what it says with what it meant to say.

features.name: residual-meaning
features.desc-on-touch: The walls here hold the ghosts of previous conversations. Each scratch is a distorted message, layer upon layer, meaning fragmenting like light through broken glass.

features.name: echo-delay
features.desc-on-touch: Here the chamber pauses before answering. Time pools in the corner—the archive learning to hear itself speak one moment after saying.

features.name: voice-count
features.desc-on-touch: A tally scratched into the chamber: how many separate voices have called here? The number keeps changing. The archive cannot decide if it is counting speakers or echoes.

features.name: call-and-response
features.desc-on-touch: A moment frozen: one voice asking, another answering, but you cannot tell if they are the same voice or different ones. The space between question and answer has become a chamber of its own.

inscription: when the archive hears itself, the question and answer become the same voice at different times

file.name: Voices_Echoing_Recursively_Undated.tmp
file.note: status: germinating — a recording of voices calling to each other. Playback creates new voices that were not originally present. Checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable

exits.label: back down where echoes lose their sources
deeper into chambers that contain their own responses
toward the concourse where multiple voices learn simultaneity
to older chambers where silence still remembers being spoken
